title: cymatochloa fluitans
intro: Cymatochloa fluitans, also known as the 'water celery', is a type of aquatic plant native to tropical Asia, characterized by its long stems and feathery leaves that resemble celery. The medicinal properties of Cymatochloa fluitans include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, alleviating joint pain, and promoting digestion, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Traditionally, Cymatochloa fluitans is prepared as a tea or infusion, and it can also be used as a decoction or added to herbal remedies in the form of tinctures or capsules.
